hero.thesis: Design eye-catching YouTube thumbnails for mid-tier creators. Charge $50–$120 per thumbnail. Secure retainer agreements for steady income.
hero.marketSize: YouTube has over 51 million channels; thousands reach monetization thresholds annually, creating demand for professional thumbnails.
hero.payingEvidence: Mid-tier creators (10k-100k subscribers) frequently outsource thumbnails, valuing the impact on click-through rates.

actions.action: Research 50 mid-tier YouTube channels across niches like gaming, beauty, tech.
actions.success: List of 50 potential clients with contact info.

actions.action: Join YouTube creator Discord servers (e.g., 'Creator Community Hub', 'YouTube Growth Lounge').
Craft personalized introduction emails using Apollo to gather additional contact info.

ninetyDay.title: Build Your Portfolio and Network

actions.action: Design 5 sample thumbnails for different niches using Canva.
actions.success: 5 polished thumbnail examples in your portfolio.

actions.action: Post your portfolio in relevant Reddit communities (e.g., r/YouTubeStartups, r/NewTubers).
Engage in LinkedIn groups for YouTube creators (e.g., 'YouTube Creators Network').

ninetyDay.metric: Portfolio shared with at least 5 interested parties.

actions.action: Follow up on initial emails sent in Week 1 with a special offer (e.g., first thumbnail at $25).
Conduct cold outreach via DMs on Discord and Reddit.
Host a free webinar on thumbnail design tips using Zoom, invite contacts.
Create a standardized retainer agreement using Notion.
Propose retainer agreements to current clients, offering a 10% discount for commitment.
Follow up with webinar attendees about retainer options.
List another 50 potential clients using your existing research methods.
Send personalized outreach to the new list.
Refine portfolio based on feedback and new designs.
actions.success: Updated portfolio with 3 new thumbnail examples.

ninetyDay.title: Optimize and Automate Workflow

actions.action: Set up workflow automation using n8n for client communications.
actions.success: Automated email sequence for client onboarding.

actions.action: Use Lovable for feedback collection from clients after each project.
Streamline design process with templates in Canva.

ninetyDay.metric: Reduce design time by 20% per thumbnail.

actions.action: Write a guest post on a relevant YouTube blog (e.g., TubeFilter, VidIQ).
Feature client success stories on your website or LinkedIn profile.
Attend a virtual YouTube marketing conference.
Implement Calendly for scheduling thumbnail feedback sessions.
Send a satisfaction survey after each project using Lovable.
Host a Q&A session with clients to gather feedback on services.
Hire a freelance designer for overflow work via platforms like Upwork.
Create a training document for freelancers using Notion.
Test collaborative design process with freelancer on 2 projects.
Analyze client acquisition data to identify best-performing channels.
Double down on high-performing channels, reduce efforts on low-performing ones.
Launch a small paid ad campaign on Instagram targeting YouTube creators.
Develop a digital course on thumbnail design basics using Teachable.
Pre-sell the course to existing clients at a discount.
Research affiliate opportunities with design tools like Canva.

ninetyDay.title: Evaluate and Plan for Future Growth

actions.action: Conduct a comprehensive review of the past 12 weeks' performance.
Set new quarterly goals based on review insights.
actions.success: New goals set for next quarter.

actions.action: Plan for hiring a part-time assistant to manage client communications.
actions.success: Assistant role defined and job post drafted.

ninetyDay.metric: Set clear objectives for the next quarter.

toolStack.name: Apollo
toolStack.why: Gather and manage leads for outreach.
toolStack.pricing: Free tier with paid plans starting at $39/month.

toolStack.name: Notion
toolStack.pricing: Free with optional Team plan at $8/user/month.

toolStack.name: n8n
toolStack.pricing: Free with optional Cloud plan at $20/month.

toolStack.name: Lovable
toolStack.pricing: Free with optional advanced features at $15/month.

firstTenCustomers.script: Hi [Name], I'm [Your Name], a YouTube thumbnail designer helping creators like you boost click-through rates with eye-catching designs. I noticed your channel [Channel Name] and believe your content could benefit from more engaging thumbnails. Let’s chat about how I can help you stand out. First thumbnail at a special rate of $25! Interested?
firstTenCustomers.cadence: Initial email/DM on Monday, follow-up on Thursday, second follow-up next Tuesday.

pricing.raisingRules: Raise prices by 10% every 6 months after securing 5 new clients, provided retention is 80%.

failureModes: Ignoring niche specialization: leads to generic portfolio.
Overcommitting to clients: causes burnout and missed deadlines.
Neglecting follow-ups: results in lost potential clients.
Pricing too low: undervalues service, reducing profit.
Ignoring client feedback: harms service improvement.
Failing to network: limits client base expansion.
Not diversifying services: stalls income growth.

setup.llc: Set up using Stripe Atlas for $500, includes bank account and tax ID.
setup.payments: Use PayPal and Stripe for easy client payments; set up within hours.
setup.contracts: Draft contracts using Notion templates for free, ensuring clear terms.
setup.insurance: Not needed initially; reconsider as you scale past $5k MRR.

scaling: To scale, consider hiring a part-time assistant to handle client communications and scheduling, freeing up your time to focus on design. As your client base grows, bring on freelance designers to manage overflow work, ensuring they are trained and aligned with your brand's quality standards. Utilize tools like Notion to centralize project management and ensure consistency. Explore additional income streams by creating a digital course or offering consulting services for creators looking to enhance their channel's visual branding. By diversifying, you stabilize your income and reduce dependency on direct client work.
